risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Participants were then randomized either to topical 1% testosterone gel (10-g daily) or placebo gel daily in 1:1 allocation during a 14-week intervention period using concealed randomization and permuted blocks with a block size of 4." Comment: This describes a random allocation process with permuted blocks and concealed randomization, which is sufficient to indicate a proper random sequence generation method under the permissive criteria for Low risk.

Quote: "Participants were then randomized either to topical 1% testosterone gel (10-g daily) or placebo gel daily in 1:1 allocation during a 14-week intervention period using concealed randomization and permuted blocks with a block size of 4." Comment: The paper explicitly states that randomization was concealed, which indicates the recruiter could not foresee the next assignment before enrollment.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The investigating staff and the participants were blinded to intervention allocation; the group and testosterone dose assignment was known only to the Investigational Drug Pharmacist... and a designated physician, who performed the dose titration." Comment: This directly states that both participants and investigating staff were blinded to allocation. Although an unblinded physician handled dose titration, this person was separate from outcome assessment and trial conduct. The main outcomes (well-being and mood questionnaires) could be influenced by knowledge of allocation, but the report indicates successful blinding of participants and personnel overall, with steps taken to maintain it.

Quote: "The investigating staff and the participants were blinded to intervention allocation" and "Well-being and mood were assessed using the Psychological General Well-Being Index (PGWBI), a 22-item questionnaire that evaluated six dimensions of self-reported wellness"; also "The Derogatis Affects Balance Scale (DABS) is a 40-item mood inventory". Comment: The outcomes in question were patient self-report questionnaires, so the participant is effectively the outcome assessor. Because both participants and staff were explicitly blinded, knowledge of allocation was unlikely to influence outcome measurement.

Quote: "In the testosterone arm, 5 were lost to follow-up, 2 withdrew due to skin irritation, 2 were discontinued due to developing hematocrit exceeding 54%, and 1 withdrew due to the smell of the gel. In the placebo arm, 11 were lost to follow up and 1 withdrew due to skin irritation. Thus, 60 and 58 participants in the testosterone and placebo arms, respectively, completed the trial." Comment: Attrition was moderate and broadly balanced (10/70 vs 12/70 overall), with similar reasons across groups and no obvious signal that dropouts were driven by the efficacy outcomes being assessed. The paper also used multiple imputation for missing outcome data, which supports a low-risk judgement.
